title: California Historic Vineyards: Interactive Data Report
excerpt: An interactive analysis and visualization of California's historic vineyards, featuring maps, variety breakdowns, and regional data from the Historic Vineyard Society registry.

title: An Exclusive Interview with Fah Sathirapongsasuti, Co-owner & Winemaker of Sunset Cellars
excerpt: Fah dives into his journey into winemaking, his philosophy, and how Sunset Cellars is crafting wines that celebrate both tradition and innovation with a deep connection to Japanese culture.

title: LGBTQ+ Leaders in Biopharma 2024
excerpt: Fah was recognized by Endpoints News as one of the LGBTQ+ Leaders in Biopharma for 2024, highlighting leaders charting a path for the industry's LGBTQ+ community.

title: Rising Stars: Queer Wine Fest Rainbow Continues to Grow in Dundee Hills
excerpt: Fah joined the second annual Queer Wine Fest at Remy Wines in Oregon's Dundee Hills, where his sparkling Sauvignon Blanc was highlighted. "Both festivals were celebrations of winemakers and the community," he said.

title: Your Genes Can Determine How Well You Dance
excerpt: Coverage of a GWAS of 606,825 individuals that identified 69 genetic loci associated with musical beat synchronization. The Vanderbilt-23andMe collaboration, with Fah as co-author, revealed shared genetics between rhythm and biological processes like walking and breathing.

title: Fah Sathirapongsasuti; Sunset Cellars — Exploring the Wine Glass Podcast
source: Dracaena Wines / Exploring the Wine Glass
excerpt: Fah's story begins in Thailand and travels through Stanford, Harvard, and 23andMe before landing at Sunset Cellars in Suisun Valley. A podcast conversation about science, wine, and finding home.

title: Scientists at Vanderbilt and 23andMe Find Genetic Link to People's Ability to Move to a Musical Beat
excerpt: Vanderbilt Genetics Institute and 23andMe researchers identified 69 genetic variants linked to musical beat synchronization in over 600,000 people, revealing connections between rhythm, brain development, and biological rhythms.

title: 'There Aren't Enough of Us': 5 Questions With Fah Sathirapongsasuti
excerpt: Winemaker and co-owner of Sunset Cellars, marketing director of the Suisun Valley Wine Co-op, PhD statistician, computer scientist and genome researcher — Fah talks about AAPI representation in wine and the #drinkAAPIwine campaign.

title: Alloy Therapeutics Acquires deepCDR Biologics to Bring Bioinformatics and Machine Learning to Antibody Discovery
excerpt: Alloy Therapeutics expanded its antibody discovery platform with the acquisition of deepCDR Biologics, bringing deeper bioinformatics, sequencing, and machine learning capabilities to the team led by Fah Sathirapongsasuti.

title: Journeys of Discovery: Exploring Suisun Valley Wine Co-Op's Sunset Cellars
excerpt: A feature on Sunset Cellars and the Suisun Valley Wine Co-Op, exploring the winery's history, its deep connection to Japanese culture, and the next chapter under Fah and his husband's stewardship.

title: Massive Study Finds No Single Genetic Cause of Same-Sex Sexual Behavior
excerpt: Fah called the study "the final nail in the coffin for Xq28" and spoke about its personal significance: "This is a very, very personal topic for me — both as a scientist and as a gay man."

title: There Is No 'Gay Gene.' There Is No 'Straight Gene.' Sexuality Is Just Complex, Study Confirms
excerpt: "As a teenager trying to understand myself and understand my sexuality, I looked at the internet for 'the gay gene' and obviously came across Xq28," said Fah, a 23andMe senior scientist and study co-author.

title: Genes Can't Predict Same-sex Sexual Behavior, Large-Scale Study Shows
excerpt: Fah spoke at the press conference for this landmark GWAS of 477,522 individuals. "We are committed to diversity, and sexual diversity is a part of that," he said.

title: There's (Still) No Gay Gene
excerpt: Harvard Magazine covered the landmark same-sex sexual behavior GWAS co-authored by Fah, a Harvard biostatistics PhD alumnus, noting that genetics accounts for 8-25% of variation in same-sex behavior.

title: There's No Such Thing as a 'Gay Gene,' Massive Study Concludes
excerpt: Fah told Live Science that "funding has historically been limited and recruitment of participants was difficult" due to the topic's controversial nature. The study found no one gene linked to same-sex behavior.

title: There's No Evidence That a Single 'Gay Gene' Exists
excerpt: "Just because something is not completely genetic or something has an environmental, or what we call nongenetic, component doesn't mean it's a choice," Fah told Science News.

title: Old People Saw White And Gold, And More Insights From a 23andMe Study of #TheDress
excerpt: Fah led a 23andMe study of 25,000 people on #TheDress. He found genetics doesn't determine what you see, but age, eye health, and even where you grew up play surprising roles in color perception.

title: The Results Are In: DNA Cannot Explain #TheDress
excerpt: "Genetics definitely doesn't determine what you see," Fah told BuzzFeed News, but his 23andMe study found that age, cataracts, and color blindness all shifted perception of the infamous dress.

title: Writer and Contributor at The Harvard Crimson
excerpt: During his PhD at Harvard, Fah contributed articles to The Harvard Crimson, the university's daily newspaper and one of the oldest continuously published newspapers in the United States.
